title: Workshop for Motivational Interviewing
tags: motivational interviewing, workshop, clinical psychology, counseling
excerpt:
I took the workshop for Motivational Interviewing by Prof. Sung-Man Shin. 
The lecture addressed ‘motivation’ based on the self-determination theory by Richard Ryan &amp; Edward Deci. 
It was interesting that the theory defines an intrinsic motiva...
content:
I took the workshop for Motivational Interviewing by Prof. Sung-Man Shin. 
The lecture addressed ‘motivation’ based on the self-determination theory by Richard Ryan &amp; Edward Deci. 
It was interesting that the theory defines an intrinsic motivation as a combination of autonomy, belonging, competence, and a sense of goal. 
After learning these theoretical backgrounds, we could practice motivation interviewing in the practice session.
I tried the role of a counselor, and I practiced the OARS techniques (Open questions, Affirming, Reflection, and Summarizing). 
I look forward to applying what I’ve learned in this workshop at the 1:1 counseling session that I’m going to do in the On-campus counseling center :)

title: Workshop for Evidence based trauma treatment
tags: Evidence-based treatment, PTSD, workshop, clinical psychology, counseling
excerpt:
I took the workshop for Evidence-based trauma treament held by Dr. Hye Sun Joo in Korea Trauma Research &amp; Education Institute. 
The lecture first addressed real examples of PTSD patients, and then discussed how to respond in certain situations...
content:
I took the workshop for Evidence-based trauma treament held by Dr. Hye Sun Joo in Korea Trauma Research &amp; Education Institute. 
The lecture first addressed real examples of PTSD patients, and then discussed how to respond in certain situations with specific exampels. 
Since I’ve never experienced PTSD patients before, this workshop will be helpful for me to work as a counselor at the on-campus counseling center.

title: Data Science Bootcamp (Math for Data Science)
tags: data science, math, workshop, linear algebra
excerpt:
From January 04 to January 11, I’ve taken the “Math for Data Science” course of the Data Science Bootcamp held by SNU Graduate School of Data Science.   
I could learn more about Linear Algebra, and I believe this would help me to better understan...
content:
From January 04 to January 11, I’ve taken the “Math for Data Science” course of the Data Science Bootcamp held by SNU Graduate School of Data Science.   
I could learn more about Linear Algebra, and I believe this would help me to better understand &amp; conduct machine learning, deep learning, and other analyses!   
Thanks to Prof. Min-hwan Oh for the wonderful lectures :)

title: Working at the on-campus counseling center
tags: clinical psychology, counseling, practice consultant, clinical experience
excerpt:
Starting this January, I’m working at the on-campus counseling center at Seoul National University, “Center for Campus Life &amp; Culture”. 
As a graduate student majoring in clinical psychology, this would be a great opportunity to experience rea...
content:
Starting this January, I’m working at the on-campus counseling center at Seoul National University, “Center for Campus Life &amp; Culture”. 
As a graduate student majoring in clinical psychology, this would be a great opportunity to experience real clinical settings, and apply what I learned in lectures so far. 
From January to June, I’ll be doing the intake interview every week :) 
I’m also going conduct some 1:1 counseling sessions! 

As a practice counselor, I hope I could learn a lot and be a helpful counselor to my future clients!

content:

content:
I gave a presentation on the paper “Gaze bias differences capture individual choice behaviour” by Thomas et al (2019). Since I am working on a project about predicting food choice and I believe that eye-tracking data contains a lot of information about an individual’s choice, the paper was fascinating. title: Paper review about Blunted ventral striatal responses to anticipated reward foreshadow problematic drug use in novelty-seeking adolescents (Buchel et al., 2017)
tags: presentation, drug use, SUD, addiction, prediction, fMRI, neuroimaging, IMAGEN
excerpt: I gave a presentation on the paper “Blunted ventral striatal responses to anticipated reward foreshadow problematic drug use in novelty-seeking adolescents” by Buchel et al. (2017). I believe predicting future drug use with longitudinal data could...
content:
I gave a presentation on the paper “Blunted ventral striatal responses to anticipated reward foreshadow problematic drug use in novelty-seeking adolescents” by Buchel et al. (2017). I believe predicting future drug use with longitudinal data could help identify the mechanism of substance use/misuses such as risk factors, and further lead to effective early interventions. Since I’m working with other large-sample longitudinal data ( ABCD project ), and looking forward to do such prediction, this paper was a great reference. Also, I hosted a class discussion in the seminar in psychopathology, and we mainly talked about “socioeconomic factors that we should consider when investigating substance use” and “effective intervention approach considering possible side effects”.

Date: 12 October, 2020  
Event: Seminar in Psychopathology 
Location: Seoul, South Korea 
Paper:  Link 
Slides:  Download pdf

title: Paper review about Neural basis of video gaming - a systematic review (Palaus et al., 2017)
tags: presentation, drug use, SUD, addiction, prediction, fMRI, neuroimaging, IMAGEN
excerpt: I gave a presentation on the paper “Neural basis of video gaming: a systematic review” by Palaus et al. (2017). Addiction is an attractive topic, in the respect of irrational decision making. So I decided to present this paper related to the “gami...
content:
I gave a presentation on the paper “Neural basis of video gaming: a systematic review” by Palaus et al. (2017). Addiction is an attractive topic, in the respect of irrational decision making. So I decided to present this paper related to the “gaming disorder”, a topic with an active discussion about whether we should define it as a mental disorder. Together with other two papers discussed in the class, we could think &amp; discuss about the issue of gaming disorder.

Date: 26 October, 2020  
Event: Seminar in Psychopathology 
Location: Seoul, South Korea

title: Presentation on Thomas et al (2020) at Clinical AI journal club
tags: presentation, machine learning, AI, Autism Spectrum Disorder, resting state fMRI, 3D-CNN, ABIDE, journal club
excerpt: I gave a presentation on “Classifying Autism Spectrum Disorder Using the Temporal Statistics of Resting-State Functional MRI Data With 3D Convolutional Neural Networks” by Thomas et al. (2020). The paper tried classifying Autism Spectrum Disorder ...
content:
I gave a presentation on “Classifying Autism Spectrum Disorder Using the Temporal Statistics of Resting-State Functional MRI Data With 3D Convolutional Neural Networks” by Thomas et al. (2020). The paper tried classifying Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) from healthy control using resting-state fMRI, with 3D Convolutional Neural Network (3D-CNN). Unlike the conventional way of spatial downscaling of fMRI data using brain atlas, the authors tried to reduce the dimensionality of time-domain using multiple summary measures of connectivity! Even though the highest prediction accuracy achieved from multi-measure ensemble approach was not high enough compared to other studies, it was a new perspective of dealing with fMRI data to me!

Date: 22 February, 2021  
Event: CCS lab meeting  
Location: Seoul, South Korea 
Paper:  Link 
Slides:  Download pdf

title: Master's degree thesis proposal presentation
tags: presentation, thesis, food addiction, binge eating disorder, obesity
excerpt: I gave a thesis proposal presentation for my master’s degree. Using the dataset being collected at 365mc hospital, I proposed a research titled “Identifying shared and unique mechanisms of food addiction and binge eating disorder based on dietary ...
content:
I gave a thesis proposal presentation for my master’s degree. Using the dataset being collected at 365mc hospital, I proposed a research titled “Identifying shared and unique mechanisms of food addiction and binge eating disorder based on dietary restriction and weight concern”. Proposal got approved, and received nice and constructive feedbacks from the committee members.
